messages.content:
“hey’ve postponed the match and it’II be played next weekend.”
The match … until next weekend.
“put” Phrasal verb needed.
The match has been put off until next weekend.

"Thomas spoke so quickly I had difficulty understanding him."
"Thomas spoke so quickly 1 .................................................. him."
"IT" What verb often goes befare 'it' and an adjective?
Thomas spoke so quickly I found it difficult to understand him.

"We phoned the restaurant, but they said booking a table wasn't necessary."
"We phoned the restaurant, but they said we .................................................. a table."
"HAVE" noun here. 
30 Use the negative form of a modal verb.
We phoned the restaurant, but they said we didn't have to book a table.

"lt seems certain that lightning started the forest fire."
The forest fire .................................................. by lightning.
HAVE / Passive verb form needed.
The forest fire is believed to have been started by lightning.

"lt wasn't worth going to the market because it was closing."
he market was closing, so ............................................... .. going there.
POINT Remember that 'point' is a noun here.
The market was closing, so there was no point in going there.

"Thomas spoke so quickly I had difficulty understanding him."
"Thomas spoke so quickly 1 .................................................. him."
What verb often goes befare 'it' and an adjective?
Thomas spoke so quickly I found it hard to understand him.
DOES "FOUND" GO BEFORE IT AND HARD?
Yes, "found" goes before "it" and "hard" in the sentence, making it: "Thomas spoke so quickly I found it hard to understand him."

(“Thomas spoke so quickly I had difficulty understanding him.”
“Thomas spoke so quickly 1 … him.”
What verb often goes befare ‘it’ and an adjective?)
DO IT IN SPANISH PLEASE
Thomas habló tan rápido que me resultó difícil entenderlo.

"“Thomas spoke so quickly I had difficulty understanding him.”
“Thomas spoke so quickly 1 … him.”
What verb often goes befare ‘it’ and an adjective?"
translate this in spanish, all of the  text

"Thomas habló tan rápido que tuve dificultades para entenderlo."
"Thomas habló tan rápido que me resultó difícil entenderlo."
"¿Qué verbo suele ir antes de ‘it’ (ello) y un adjetivo?"
